trivia_bullets.text: Part of the first-ever cycle of wedge-color three-color Charms in Magic history. Mark Rosewater's design handoff for KTK notes: 'We have made monocolor charms, two-color charms, and three-color charms but never three-color charms in the wedges' colors.' Released 2014-09-26.
sources: Wizards: Khans of Tarkir Design Handoff Part 2 (magic.wizards.com)

trivia_bullets.text: The Mardu Horde's clan identity is speed (dragon aspect: wing). Their stated philosophy — 'Those who ride will conquer, while those who stop to meditate will die' — maps onto the charm's three modes: burn, token warriors, hand disruption.
sources: Wizards: Planeswalker's Guide to Khans of Tarkir Part 2 (magic.wizards.com)

trivia_bullets.text: Illustrated by Mathias Kollros, an Austrian fantasy illustrator who debuted in Magic with Return to Ravnica (2012). No flavor text printed on this card.
EDHREC rank 16,827; approximately 3,136 Commander decks. Most common home is Edgar Markov and Kaalia of the Vast lists seeking low-cost modal utility.

related_cards.name: Abzan Charm
related_cards.note: Five-clan KTK charm cycle member; considered the strongest

related_cards.name: Jeskai Charm
related_cards.note: Five-clan KTK charm cycle member; trivia-passed wave 176

related_cards.name: Mardu Roughrider
related_cards.note: Same clan, same set, Mardu watermark

flags.ktk_charm_cycle_cohort_trigger: Mardu Charm is the 2nd of 5 KTK charms to receive trivia (Jeskai wave 176, Mardu this pass). Trigger condition for 'KTK five-clan charm cycle' cohort node: 3 of 5 needed. Temur and Sultai/Abzan still pending.
